pool.baseline:
Hey, I'm looking at the audit from the Rotterdam hub for the Q3 electronic component stock. We've got some weird discrepancies between our WMS and the physical count. Can you look at this data and tell me why we might be seeing these small gaps?

Item ID | Expected | Physical | Variance
EC-992-X | 1,450 | 1,447 | -3
EC-441-B | 800 | 799 | -1
EC-102-Z | 2,200 | 2,196 | -4
EC-558-L | 500 | 498 | -2

It happens every month with these small amounts. Just looking for potential causes like logging lags or unit of measure errors.

pool.baseline:
hey i keep getting a 500 error on the /api/v1/checkout/process endpoint whenever a user has multiple items in their cart with different discount codes applied. here are the logs from the last few occurrences:

[2023-10-27 14:22:01] ERROR: Unexpected null pointer in DiscountService.calculateTotal()
[2023-10-27 14:22:01] STACKTRACE: com.ecommerce.service.DiscountService.applyPromotions(DiscountService.java:142)
[2023-10-27 14:25:12] ERROR: Atomic operation failed: Database transaction timed out during lock acquisition
[2023-10-27 14:25:12] STACKTRACE: com.ecommerce.db.TransactionManager.commit(TransactionManager.java:89)

seems like it might be a race condition or something with the logic handling the stack of applied promos, but i cant tell for sure. what's causing this?
